Worktree/ux docs release (#31)

* feat: add GET /metrics endpoint for session statistics (B2)

Add in-memory session metrics tracking per-encounter win/loss rates,
average rounds, token balance snapshots (start/end), total deaths,
and resource inflow/outflow (Ore, Plant, Lumber, Fish).

- New EncounterRecord type tracks discipline, outcome, rounds, and
  token snapshots per encounter
- SessionMetrics computed on demand from encounter records
- Metrics reset on NewGame
- All finish_*_encounter methods now use record_encounter_finish()
  helper for consistent tracking
- Token snapshots captured at encounter start and finish

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add /docs/tutorial, /docs/hints, /docs/designer endpoints with tests

Add three self-documenting API endpoints:
- GET /docs/tutorial: 8-step new-player walkthrough
- GET /docs/hints: strategies and tips per discipline (all 8)
- GET /docs/designer: encounter templates, card kinds, token
  lifecycles, effect system, balance levers, config reference

All endpoints return structured JSON with OpenAPI schemas.
Integration tests (10 tests) cover structure validation,
discipline coverage, and metrics accumulation/reset.

GET /player/tokens, GET /actions/log. Also document all PlayerActions enum
variants with their strategic purpose.

BREAKING: remove target from LoseTokens, convert OnSelf costs to rolled_costs (#30)

Remove the `target` field from `CardEffectKind::LoseTokens` — LoseTokens
always implicitly targets the opponent. Add `card_value: Option<i64>` to
`ConcreteEffect` for transparent cost calculation.

Convert all LoseTokens(OnSelf) cost patterns across 5 gathering disciplines
(mining, woodcutting, crafting, fishing, herbalism) to use the rolled_costs
model on benefit effects. Each discipline now computes a card_value from its
benefit effects and stores costs as percentages of that value.

Key changes:
- types.rs: Remove target from LoseTokens variant; add card_value to ConcreteEffect
- game_state.rs: Rewrite extract_gathering_costs_from_effects to read rolled_costs;
  update check_and_deduct_costs/preview_costs to use card_value
- combat.rs: Update apply_card_effects to determine LoseTokens target implicitly
- mining.rs: Create 3 new GainTokens-with-costs templates; update all card compositions
- woodcutting.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; rewrite all 12 card compositions
- crafting.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; update 3 card compositions
- fishing.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; rewrite all 12 card compositions
- herbalism.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; rewrite all 9 card compositions
- action/mod.rs: Update 3 extract_gathering_costs_from_effects call sites

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add Balancing track (B1-B7) to roadmap and expand vision balancing section (#29)

BREAKING: Replaces roadmap Step 16 (vague balancing placeholder) with a
detailed 7-step Balancing track (B1-B7) that leverages the game's
architecture (in-memory, deterministic, REST API) for automated balance
testing via headless Monte Carlo simulation + LLM analysis.

Balancing track summary:
- B1: Multi-instance port configuration for parallel runs
- B2: GET /metrics endpoint for session statistics
- B3: Python headless simulation runner with scripted strategies
- B4: Baseline measurement and first balance pass
- B5: LLM analysis pipeline for balance suggestions
- B6: Strategy variety validation (3+ strategies per discipline)
- B7: Continuous balance regression (make balance-check)

Vision updates:
- Added 'Continuous playability' high-level principle
- Added explicit win-rate targets (80% easy, 50% hard, 20-30% milestones)
- Expanded tuning pipeline with headless Monte Carlo and LLM analysis
- Added synergy note to Step 14 (config externalization → balance tooling)

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>

used for delta computation (previous improved experiment) instead of
always showing original baseline. The column header dynamically shows
'Experiment N' or 'Baseline' as appropriate. Per-scenario breakdowns

fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Fix unstuck PR loop to advance draft PRs instead of incorrectly skipping them (#609)

* Initial plan

* Fix unstuck PR loop to use check_pr_merge_status like advance_issue_pr

The main PR loop in run_unstuck only called has_failing_checks_for_pr(),
which returns false for draft PRs with no checks, incorrectly logging
"checks OK, skipping". Now uses check_pr_merge_status() to properly
handle draft PRs (marking them ready for review), agent WIP, not-yet-
mergeable PRs (checking failures, approving workflows, enabling auto-
merge), and mergeable PRs — matching the advancement logic already used
in advance_issue_pr.

commit.message:
chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow (#154)

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls
the pinned image on the VPS, retags it as :latest, and restarts via
docker compose — without touching .env or docker-compose.yml.

Shares the deploy-vps concurrency group to prevent concurrent
deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add deploy-vps concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml

The rollback workflow used group: deploy-vps but no existing deploy
workflow shared that group, so concurrent deploy + rollback runs were
not actually blocked.

Adding the same concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml (the reusable
workflow called by both deploy.yml and release.yml) means all VPS
deployments now share the group with rollback.yml, enforcing real
mutual exclusion.

Addresses PR review comment: concurrency group not shared with existing deploy workflows

dimensions.

* fix: add SetEnvKeyReplacer so nested APP_* env vars are read correctly

Viper's AutomaticEnv() upper-cases keys as-is, producing APP_DATABASE.HOST
(with a literal dot) instead of APP_DATABASE_HOST. Without a replacer, the
env var lookup always fails and the default value (localhost) is used.
